intended_use: The UltraSight AI Guidance is intended to assist medical professionals (not including expert sonographers) in acquiring cardiac ultrasound images. UltraSight AI Guidance is an accessory to compatible
intended_use_full: The UltraSight AI Guidance is intended to assist medical professionals (not including expert sonographers) in acquiring cardiac ultrasound images. UltraSight AI Guidance is an accessory to compatible general-purpose diagnostic ultrasound systems. UltraSight AI Guidance is indicated for use in two-dimensional transthoracic echocardiography (2D-TTE) for adult patients, specifically in the acquisition of the following standard views: Parasternal Long-Axis (PLAX), Parasternal Short-Axis at the Aortic Valve (PSAX-AV), Parasternal Short-Axis at the Mitral Valve (PSAX-MV), Parasternal Short-Axis at the Papillary Muscle (PSAX-PM), Apical 4-Chamber (AP4), Apical 5-Chamber (AP5), Apical 2-Chamber (AP2), Apical 3-Chamber (AP3), Subcostal 4-Chamber (SubC4), and Subcostal Inferior Vena Cava (SC-IVC).
target_condition: Cardiac assessment requiring two-dimensional transthoracic echocardiography
ground_truth: Expert cardiologists (panel of 5) blinded to acquisition method assessed clip diagnosability and diagnostic parameters; ground truth annotations by expert sonographers for algorithm testing
other_metrics: Quality Bar AUC: 0.86 [95% CI 0.85-0.87]; View Detection AUC: 0.988 [95% CI 0.985-0.990]; Probe Guidance AUC: 0.821 [95% CI 0.813-0.827]; Pivotal study primary endpoints: 93-100% of non-expert user scans had adequate visual quality for assessment of LV size, LV function, RV size, and pericardial effusion; Secondary endpoints: 67-98% adequate visual quality across six additional parameters; 78.6-97.9% of clips had diagnostic quality score of 3 or higher on ACEP scale
